fields.description:
## What
The `rebar metrics` subcommand: parses `--since/--until` and `--output json|text`, renders the full metric registry over the range, and is the single entry point wiring the readers. (Date bucketing is intentionally out of scope for this ticket — the command reports each metric over the whole `[since, until]` range; a per-bucket `--bucket` series can be a later additive enhancement and is NOT part of this ticket's contract.)

## Files
- `src/rebar/_commands/metrics.py` — new: arg parsing + a render loop that evaluates every `MetricSpec` in the registry.
- `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` — wire the `metrics` READ subcommand with a single mechanism: add an explicit `if sub == "metrics":` arm in `_dispatch()` (mirroring `init`/`scratch`) that imports `rebar._commands.metrics` and calls its entry point, placed on the reads path that first ensures the store is initialized (as the other read commands are). This is ONE dispatch arm — not a frozenset-membership route (no existing frozenset carries it).
- `src/rebar/_cli/help/metrics.txt` — new help text.
- `src/rebar/_cli/help/overview.txt` — add the `metrics` line to the subcommand overview.
- `tests/golden/cli_help/*.out` — regenerate the overview snapshots (`__overview_help.out`, `__overview_dashhelp.out`, and the other overview goldens) and add `metrics__help.out`; the subcommand set is enumerated there.
- `docs/cli-reference.md` — do NOT hand-edit: it is GENERATED by `scripts/gen_cli_reference.py` (with a CI drift gate). After adding the help/overview text, REGENERATE it (`python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py`) and commit the result.
- `tests/interfaces/contracts/test_help_overview_coverage.py` — this drift guard checks that every routable subcommand is advertised in the overview and has pinned help text. Its `_routable_subcommands()` unions the dispatch frozensets PLUS an `individual` frozenset listing commands routed by an explicit `if sub == …` arm (`init`, `scratch`, `delete`, `fsck`, …). Because `metrics` is routed by such an explicit arm (not a frozenset), **add `"metrics"` to that `individual` frozenset** so `test_routable_set_matches_pinned_help_set` counts it routable; the new `help/metrics.txt` and the `overview.txt` `metrics` line satisfy the pinned-help and advertised-in-overview halves of the guard.
- `tests/interfaces/test_metrics_cli.py` — new CLI subprocess test.

## Approach
A thin CLI over the registry keeps the command complete-once: it builds a **context object** and calls `rebar.metrics.registry.evaluate(spec, context)` for every `MetricSpec`, emitting value-or-`unavailable` per metric, so new signals appear with no CLI change.

**REGISTRY hydration (import the PACKAGE, not just registry):** `REGISTRY` is only fully populated after the reader modules run their registration side-effects, and those imports live in `src/rebar/metrics/__init__.py` (which imports `event_metrics`, `git_metrics`, `sidecar_metrics`). Importing `rebar.metrics.registry` ALONE does not trigger them. So `metrics.py` must `import rebar.metrics` (the package — running its `__init__`, which hydrates `REGISTRY`) and then read `rebar.metrics.registry.REGISTRY`. This does NOT breach adapter-isolation: `__init__.py` imports the reader modules but NOT `rebar.metrics.adapters.*`.

**The context object (the contract the readers already expect):** every reader's registered `compute` is a single-arg callable that reads `ctx.repo_root`, `ctx.since`, `ctx.until` (via `getattr(ctx, "repo_root", None)` etc.). So `metrics.py` constructs a small context (a dataclass or `SimpleNamespace`) carrying exactly `repo_root` (the resolved store root), `since`, and `until` (the parsed range), and passes it to `evaluate`.

**Adapter isolation (portability guard from 1f77/538c):** `metrics.py` must NOT import any `rebar.metrics.adapters` module. The guard is a **source-level scan**: the test reads `src/rebar/_commands/metrics.py` and asserts its source contains no `rebar.metrics.adapters` import (holds regardless of whether the adapters package exists). Adapter-backed data reaches the command only via `REGISTRY` / the snapshot store, never a direct import.

**Field sourcing (spec vs value).** `evaluate(spec, ctx)` returns either a `MetricValue` (carrying `.value`, `.source`, `.confidence` — but NOT `lens`) or an `Unavailable` (`.reason`, `.accruing_since`). The `lens` lives on the `MetricSpec`, so the render loop composes each entry from BOTH: `lens = spec.lens`, and value/source/confidence/reason from the `evaluate` result. (Do not read `.lens` off `MetricValue` — it has none.)

**Fault isolation.** `registry.evaluate()` does NOT catch exceptions raised inside a metric's `compute` (e.g. a reader hitting a git/IO error). So the render loop wraps each `evaluate(spec, ctx)` in `try/except`: on an exception it renders that metric as `unavailable` (reason = a short error string) rather than letting one failing metric crash the whole command. Every registered id therefore always appears in the output.

**Output formats.** JSON (default) is the machine contract: `{"since": <str>, "until": <str>, "metrics": {<id>: {"lens","source","confidence","value"} | {"unavailable": {"reason","accruing_since"}}, ...}}` where `lens` comes from the spec and `source`/`confidence`/`value` from the `MetricValue`. `--output text` is a deterministic human summary — one line per metric in registry order: `<id>  [<lens>/<source>]  <value-or-"unavailable: <reason>">` (a self-defined layout, NOT borrowed from `rebar audit`). Both formats include every registered metric id.

## Tests
- CLI (real subprocess against a temp store): `rebar metrics --since ... --until ... --output json` exits 0 and emits a `metrics` map containing every registered id; an unpopulated metric shows `unavailable`; `--output text` lists every metric id.
- **Test-side REGISTRY hydration:** the test module must `import rebar.metrics` (the package, running its `__init__` reader imports) BEFORE reading `REGISTRY`, and assert `len(REGISTRY) > 0` first — otherwise the `set(output) >= {s.id for s in REGISTRY}` comparison could be vacuously true against an unpopulated `REGISTRY` in the test process. (The subprocess-run command hydrates its own REGISTRY the same way via `metrics.py`.)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/interfaces/test_metrics_cli.py` passes: `rebar metrics --since 2026-01-01 --until 2026-07-01 --output json` exits 0 and the emitted `.metrics` map contains an entry for EVERY id in `rebar.metrics.registry.REGISTRY`. The test FIRST asserts `REGISTRY` is non-empty (`assert {s.id for s in REGISTRY}`), THEN `set(output["metrics"].keys()) >= {s.id for s in REGISTRY}` (so the `>=` is not vacuously true), each entry being either a value (with a `lens` from the spec plus `source`+`confidence`+`value`) or an `unavailable` object with a non-empty `reason`.
- [ ] `--output text` prints one line per registered metric id (a human summary) — CLI test asserts every id appears and an unavailable metric renders as `unavailable`.
- [ ] the portability guard holds: a test asserts `src/rebar/_commands/metrics.py`'s source does not import `rebar.metrics.adapters`.
- [ ] `rebar metrics --help` renders the help file and the command is listed by bare `rebar` — CLI test asserts exit 0 and help contains `--since`.
- [ ] the golden CLI-help snapshots are regenerated (and `docs/cli-reference.md` via `gen_cli_reference.py`), and the `tests/golden/cli_help` + help-overview-coverage suites pass with `metrics` listed.
